 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ep21.2","title":"fcp.asana: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ep21.3","title":"TEST: fcp.asana Unit/Integration Tests (mock-only)","description":"## Goal
Mock-only unit/integration tests for this connector.

## Required coverage
- schema validation (inputs + outputs)
- error taxonomy mapping (retry semantics)
- pagination and bounds
- rate limit behavior
- redaction (no token/secret/PII leakage)
- idempotency + receipts for Dangerous operations

## Logging
All tests emit structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic tests; no live external calls.
- Includes adversarial cases (timeouts, malformed responses, partial failures).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ep21.4","title":"E2E: fcp.asana Connector Compliance Run","description":"## Goal
Run the shared connector compliance runner and capture an evidence bundle.

## Must validate
- handshake + introspection correctness
- default deny (no CapabilityToken → deny + DecisionReceipt)
- NetworkConstraints enforcement
- receipts/audit for Dangerous operations
- structured logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- E2E run is deterministic and produces a stable artifact bundle.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-epqh","title":"FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework","description":"# FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework

## Goal
Define and maintain a rigorous prioritization framework for the connector portfolio (value, risk, effort, dependencies) so roadmap decisions are coherent, explainable, and user-focused.

## Executive Summary

This document captures the strategic thinking, prioritization rationale, and implementation roadmap for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP) connector portfolio. The goal is to maximize agent autonomy and capability while building a sustainable, high-quality connector ecosystem.

---

## Strategic Vision

### The Flywheel Effect
Connectors create a flywheel of value:
1. **More connectors** → Agents can do more things
2. **More capable agents** → More users adopt the platform
3. **More users** → More demand for connectors
4. **More demand** → Justification for building more connectors

### Design Principles
- **Quality over quantity**: Each connector should be production-ready, not a proof-of-concept
- **Ecosystem coherence**: Connectors should work together, enabling cross-service workflows
- **Security by default**: Default-deny, capability-based permissions throughout
- **Future-proof**: FCP2 spec compliance ensures consistency and upgradeability

---

## Prioritization Framework

### Value Assessment Model
Each connector is scored on five dimensions:

| Dimension | Weight | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **User Reach** | 25% | How many users need this service |
| **Agent Autonomy** | 25% | How much more can agents do with this |
| **Flywheel Synergy** | 20% | Integration potential with other connectors |
| **Strategic Position** | 15% | Competitive differentiation |
| **Implementation Cost** | 15% | Effort vs. payoff ratio |

### Tier Definitions

#### Tier 1: Critical Infrastructure (Score 93-100)
**Build immediately. Foundation for everything else.**

These connectors either:
- Enable the core agent workflow loop
- Provide capabilities no other connector can substitute
- Have massive user reach (>50% of target users)

Connectors:
- `fcp.twitter` (98) — Real-time social layer, high visibility
- `fcp.linear` (97) — Modern issue tracking, Beads sync
- `fcp.stripe` (96) — Payments/billing, revenue operations
- `fcp.youtube` (95) — Video/transcript access, research
- `fcp.browser` (95) — Universal fallback, any website
- `fcp.plaid` (94) — Banking/financial data
- `fcp.anthropic` (94) — Core AI provider
- `fcp.openai` (94) — Broadest AI capabilities
- `fcp.google-ai` (94) — Massive context, multimodal
- `fcp.vectordb` (93) — Memory/search infrastructure

#### Tier 2: High-Value Productivity (Score 83-92)
**Build in phase 2. Major capability expansion.**

These connectors:
- Serve large user segments
- Enable important workflow categories
- Have strong synergies with Tier 1

Connectors:
- `fcp.microsoft365` (91) — Enterprise productivity suite
- `fcp.jira` (89) — Enterprise issue tracking
- `fcp.twilio` (88) — Communications infrastructure
- `fcp.hubspot` (87) — CRM/marketing automation
- `fcp.airtable` (86) — No-code database
- `fcp.figma` (85) — Design collaboration
- `fcp.sentry` (85) — Error tracking/debugging
- `fcp.datadog` (84) — Observability platform
- `fcp.grafana` (83) — Open-source observability

#### Tier 3: Specialized Value (Score 70-82)
**Build in phase 3. Domain-specific capabilities.**

These connectors:
- Serve specific user segments very well
- Enable specialized workflows
- May be safety-critical (credentials, infrastructure)

Connectors:
- `fcp.kubernetes` (82) — Container orchestration
- `fcp.s3` (81) — Object storage
- `fcp.terraform` (80) — Infrastructure as code
- `fcp.1password` (79) — Credential management
- `fcp.arxiv` (78) — Research papers
- `fcp.zendesk` (77) — Customer support
- `fcp.semanticscholar` (77) — Citation analysis
- `fcp.annas-archive` (76) — Book/document search
- `fcp.reddit` (76) — Community discussions
- `fcp.docusign` (75) — E-signatures
- `fcp.homeassistant` (74) — Home automation
- `fcp.spotify` (72) — Music/podcast

#### Meta-Connectors: Force Multipliers
**Build throughout. Infrastructure for the infrastructure.**

These aren't service connectors but platform capabilities:
- `fcp.webhook-receiver` (88) — Inbound event gateway
- `fcp.cron` (86) — Scheduled task runner
- `fcp.mcp-bridge` — MCP server passthrough

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Synergy Map

```
                    Agent Mail (coordination hub)
                           ↑
         ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────┐
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
    AI Providers     Data Sources      Communication
    (Anthropic,      (GitHub,          (Slack, Twilio,
     OpenAI,          Linear,           Discord,
     Google AI)       Stripe)           Email)
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
         └─────────────────┼─────────────────┘
                           ↓
    ┌──────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
    ↓                      ↓                      ↓
 Vector DB            CASS/CM                   BV
 (memory)          (search/memory)       (task tracking)
```

### Key Integration Patterns

#### 1. Development Workflow
```
GitHub PR → Linear issue → Agent investigates →
  → Sentry errors + Datadog metrics →
  → Agent proposes fix → PR merged → Linear updated
```

#### 2. Customer Success Workflow
```
Zendesk ticket → Agent analyzes →
  → Searches knowledge base → Drafts response →
  → If complex: creates Linear issue →
  → Updates HubSpot contact record
```

#### 3. Revenue Operations Workflow
```
Stripe payment event → Agent correlates →
  → Updates HubSpot deal → Sends Twilio notification →
  → If failed: creates Zendesk ticket →
  → Generates report in Airtable
```

#### 4. Research Workflow
```
User question → Agent searches ArXiv + Semantic Scholar →
  → Downloads papers → AI summarizes →
  → Stores in Vector DB → Returns synthesis
```

---

## Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4)
**Focus: Core agent capabilities**

- AI Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI
- Meta: Webhook Receiver, Cron
- Critical: Twitter, Linear, GitHub
- Storage: Vector DB, S3

### Phase 2: Productivity (Weeks 5-8)
**Focus: Business workflows**

- Enterprise: Microsoft 365, Jira
- Communication: Twilio, Slack
- CRM: HubSpot
- Observability: Sentry, Datadog, Grafana
- No-code: Airtable
- Design: Figma
- Payments: Stripe, Plaid

### Phase 3: Specialization (Weeks 9-12)
**Focus: Domain expertise**

- Infrastructure: Kubernetes, Terraform
- Research: ArXiv, Semantic Scholar, Anna's Archive
- Support: Zendesk
- Legal: DocuSign
- Security: 1Password

### Phase 4: Expansion (Weeks 13+)
**Focus: Long-tail value**

- Social: Reddit
- Media: Spotify, YouTube
- Home: Home Assistant
- Additional based on demand

---

## Quality Standards

### Every Connector Must:
1. **Pass FCP2 compliance runner** — Static and dynamic checks
2. **Have no real API calls in tests** — Mock-only unit tests
3. **Implement proper zones** — Capability-based security
4. **Support audit logging** — All operations tracked
5. **Handle rate limits** — Respect service quotas
6. **Document synergies** — Integration patterns explicit

### Bead Standards
Each connector bead must include:
- Strategic value score with justification
- Concrete agent use cases (not abstract)
- Flywheel synergies with specific connectors
- Zone architecture recommendations
- Capability model (read/write/admin)
- Representative operations with examples
- Testing approach
- Acceptance criteria

---

## Risk Considerations

### API Stability
- Services may change APIs
- Version pinning where possible
- Deprecation monitoring needed

### Rate Limits
- Different per service
- Budget enforcement at zone level
- Queue management for bursts

### Security
- Credentials never written to disk
- 1Password as credential source
- Audit trails mandatory

### Cost
- Some APIs charge per call
- AI providers charge per token
- Budget controls essential

---

## Success Metrics

### Coverage Metrics
- % of target workflows enabled
- # of connectors at each tier
- Cross-connector workflow coverage

### Quality Metrics
- FCP2 compliance rate
- Test coverage per connector
- API error rates

### Usage Metrics
- Operations per connector
- Workflow completion rates
- User satisfaction scores

---

## Appendix: Connector Index by Category

### AI Providers
- fcp.anthropic, fcp.openai, fcp.google-ai

### Communication
- fcp.slack, fcp.discord, fcp.telegram, fcp.twilio

### Development
- fcp.github, fcp.linear, fcp.jira

### Observability
- fcp.sentry, fcp.datadog, fcp.grafana

### Productivity
- fcp.microsoft365, fcp.notion, fcp.airtable

### CRM/Sales
- fcp.hubspot, fcp.stripe

### Financial
- fcp.plaid, fcp.stripe

### Research
- fcp.arxiv, fcp.semanticscholar, fcp.annas-archive

### Infrastructure
- fcp.kubernetes, fcp.terraform, fcp.s3

### Social
- fcp.twitter, fcp.reddit, fcp.youtube

### Support
- fcp.zendesk, fcp.intercom

### Security
- fcp.1password, fcp.bitwarden

### Design
- fcp.figma

### Legal
- fcp.docusign, fcp.pandadoc

### Personal
- fcp.spotify, fcp.homeassistant

### Meta-Connectors
- fcp.webhook-receiver, fcp.cron, fcp.browser

## Success Criteria
- A stable scoring model exists (value, risk, effort, dependencies) and is applied consistently across the connector list.
- Tiering/prioritization outputs are actionable: they produce concrete connector epic work with dependencies that align to the FCP2 platform build order.
- The framework can be updated incrementally without losing rationale (future maintainers can understand why rankings changed).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eqb","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide streaming access to Home Assistant’s event bus via WebSocket:
- state_changed
- call_service
- custom events

## Scope
- Establish websocket session and authenticate.
- Subscribe to events with filters (domain/entity allowlists).
- Deliver events as FCP2 event envelopes with:
  - stable event IDs
  - correlation IDs

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.events.subscribe` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned (WS + HTTPS to HA host only).
- Streaming must be bounded/backpressured:
  - cap queue depth
  - drop/deny with explicit reason codes when overloaded
- Taint:
  - event payloads are tainted external input

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `ha.events.subscribe`
- Event envelope types:
  - `ha.event.state_changed`
  - `ha.event.call_service`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - reconnect + resubscribe
  - bounds/backpressure behavior
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Event streaming is reliable under reconnects and never causes unbounded memory growth.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","title":"TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests (Availability Tracking, Pre-staging, Coverage Metrics)","description":"# TEST: Offline Capability Unit Tests

## Goal
Comprehensive unit tests for offline capability tracking from V2 spec section 21.

## Test Categories

### OfflineAccess Tests
- can_access() returns true when local_symbols >= k
- can_access() returns false when local_symbols < k
- coverage() calculates correct ratio (local_symbols / k)
- Edge cases: k=0, local_symbols=0, overflow

### OfflineCapability Tests
- Accessible map tracks multiple objects correctly
- Object removal updates capability state
- Query by zone returns correct subset
- Memory efficient for large object counts

### Pre-staging Tests
- Access pattern tracking updates correctly
- Frequent objects prioritized for pre-staging
- Pre-staging respects device storage limits
- Stale access patterns decay appropriately
- Pre-staging triggers before predicted access

### Integration with Stores
- Symbol store updates reflect in OfflineAccess
- Symbol eviction updates can_access() correctly
- Repair loop improves coverage metrics

## Golden Vectors
- Location: tests/golden/offline_capability/
- Include: OfflineAccess serialization, coverage calculations

## Logging Requirements (per 1n78.35)
All tests emit structured JSON:
{
  "timestamp": "RFC3339",
  "test": "can_access_sufficient_symbols",
  "object_id": "...",
  "local_symbols": 60,
  "k": 50,
  "expected_can_access": true,
  "result": "pass|fail"
}

## Fuzz Targets
- OfflineAccess from arbitrary symbol counts
- Pre-staging priority calculation

## Acceptance Criteria
- All can_access/coverage calculations correct
- Pre-staging improves offline availability
- Integration with stores is consistent","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:41:38.187529965-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T02:42:07.112633034-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-16T02:42:07.112633034-05:00","close_reason":"26 comprehensive unit tests covering all requirements: OfflineAccess, OfflineCapability, AccessPatternTracker with edge cases","labels":["fcp2","mvp","storage","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-31c7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:41:52.157161918-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-f3xi","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:41:52.29944295-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-f50","title":"fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting","description":"# fcp.reddit: User Posting and Commenting (FCP2)

## Goal
Enable controlled Reddit participation:
- submit posts
- submit comments
- edit/delete own content

These are real-world side effects and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Submit post to a subreddit (text/link).
- Submit comment.
- Edit content.
- Delete content.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `reddit.post.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.comment.write` (dangerous)
- `reddit.content.edit` (dangerous)

Policy guidance:
- Posting should often require ApprovalToken depending on zone policy.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Content safety:
  - outbound content must be treated as a “publication” action
  - receipts + audit events include subreddit + post/comment id
  - never log post bodies

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `reddit.post.submit`
  - `reddit.comment.submit`
  - `reddit.content.edit`
  - `reddit.content.delete`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - request formation
  - approval gating
  - audit/receipt emission
  - error mapping (subreddit rules, forbidden)

## Acceptance criteria
- Write operations are mechanically authorized, explainable, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-n6r","title":"fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval)","description":"# fcp.terraform: Safety-critical design enforcement (Plan=read-only, Apply=approval) (FCP2)

## Goal
Make Terraform operations mechanically safe:
- plan is always read-only
- apply is always approval-gated and bound to a specific plan hash
- destroy is forbidden by default

This bead defines the safety envelope that all other terraform operations must obey.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Core safety rules (non-negotiable)
- Never run Terraform with `-auto-approve`.
- Always run with `-input=false`.
- Apply requires:
  - a previously generated plan reference
  - a matching plan hash
  - an ApprovalToken when policy requires
- Explicit denial reason codes for:
  - missing plan
  - hash mismatch
  - drift detected
  - destroy forbidden

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `terraform.plan`
- `terraform.apply` (dangerous; approval required)
- `terraform.destroy` (critical; disabled unless explicitly enabled)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- Sandbox profile must:
  - restrict filesystem to workspace allowlist
  - restrict process execution to terraform binary
- Audit:
  - all state reads are audited
  - all applies produce receipts + audit events

## Deliverables
- Safety gate layer used by all terraform operations.
- Canonical DecisionReceipt reason codes for denials.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Unit tests for:
  - refusal of auto-approve
  - refusal of apply without plan
  - hash mismatch denial
  - destroy forbidden default
  - audit event emission on state reads

## Acceptance criteria
- It is mechanically impossible to apply/destroy without explicit authorization.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nlz4","title":"TEST: Capabilities Unit/Adversarial Tests (Token Verification, Grant Enforcement, Holder Proof)","description":"# TEST: Capabilities Unit/Adversarial Tests

## Goal
Prove that the capability system is airtight - no unauthorized operations can execute.

## Test Categories

### COSE_Sign1 Token Parsing
- [ ] Valid token parses and verifies
- [ ] Malformed CBOR rejected
- [ ] Invalid signature rejected
- [ ] Wrong issuer key rejected
- [ ] Missing protected headers rejected
- [ ] KID extraction and lookup works

### Token Field Validation
- [ ] Missing required fields rejected (jti, sub, iss_zone, iss_node, aud, iat, exp)
- [ ] Invalid field types rejected
- [ ] Time validation: expired token rejected
- [ ] Time validation: future iat beyond skew rejected
- [ ] aud mismatch rejected (wrong connector_id)
- [ ] aud_binary required for Risky/Dangerous - rejected if missing
- [ ] holder_node required for Risky/Dangerous - rejected if missing

### Grant Verification (grant_object_ids)
- [ ] Empty grant_object_ids rejected
- [ ] Referenced grant object not found → rejection
- [ ] Revoked grant object → rejection
- [ ] Token caps NOT subset of grant union → rejection
- [ ] Token caps IS subset of grant union → allow
- [ ] Attenuation that EXPANDS authority → rejection
- [ ] Attenuation that RESTRICTS authority → allow

### Checkpoint Freshness (chk_id/chk_seq)
- [ ] Missing chk_id/chk_seq rejected (for Risky/Dangerous)
- [ ] chk_id mismatch (wrong checkpoint) → rejection
- [ ] chk_seq > local checkpoint_seq → degraded mode or rejection per policy
- [ ] chk_seq <= local checkpoint_seq → allow

### Holder Proof Verification
- [ ] holder_node present but holder_proof missing → rejection
- [ ] holder_proof with wrong signable bytes → rejection
- [ ] holder_proof signed by wrong key → rejection
- [ ] holder_proof valid → allow
- [ ] Signable bytes include: request_id, operation_id, token.jti

### Issuer Verification
- [ ] Issuer node has valid NodeKeyAttestation → allow
- [ ] Issuer attestation expired → rejection
- [ ] Issuer attestation revoked → rejection
- [ ] Issuer not authorized for iss_zone → rejection

### Role Resolution
- [ ] RoleAssignment resolves to effective capabilities
- [ ] Role inheritance traverses DAG correctly
- [ ] Cyclic role inheritance detected and rejected
- [ ] Role attenuation restricts correctly

### Attack Scenarios (Adversarial)
- [ ] Token replay from different request_id rejected (via holder_proof)
- [ ] Token from revoked issuer rejected
- [ ] Token referencing revoked grant rejected
- [ ] Stale checkpoint attack: old token with old checkpoint accepted/rejected per policy
- [ ] Audience confusion: token for connector A used against connector B rejected
- [ ] Binary binding: token without aud_binary used for Dangerous op rejected

## Golden Vectors
- Valid token bytes with all fields → successful verification
- Each attack scenario → specific reason_code

## Fuzz Targets
- COSE_Sign1 parsing
- Grant subset verification
- Role inheritance resolution

## Logging Requirements
```json
{
  "test_name": "...",
  "token_jti": "...",
  "verification_step": "signature|grants|freshness|holder|issuer",
  "result": "pass|fail",
  "reason_code": "...",
  "evidence_ids": [...]
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- Every verification step has both positive and negative tests
- Attack scenarios are documented and tested
- Fuzz targets find no bypasses

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-nnja","title":"TEST: Credential Injection Unit Tests (SecretObject, CredentialObject, Egress Injection, Zeroize)","description":"# TEST: Credential Injection Unit Tests (SecretObject, CredentialObject, Egress Injection, Zeroize)

## Goal
Prove (mechanically) that the platform’s credential model is safe and enforceable:
- secret bytes do not leak
- credential usage is capability-gated
- egress proxy injection is correct
- audits/DecisionReceipts are emitted and actionable

This bead is the test owner for `flywheel_connectors-kt9r`.

## Scope
Tests cover:
- `SecretObject` + `SecretAccessToken` semantics (authorization, reconstruction hooks, audit)
- `CredentialObject` validation + host binding
- `CapabilityConstraints.credential_allow` enforcement
- Egress proxy credential injection behavior (HTTP headers, optional TCP/TLS)

## Test Categories

### 1) CredentialObject validation (unit)
- [ ] `CredentialId` format constraints (canonical, stable)
- [ ] `CredentialApply` modes validated (HttpHeader/QueryParam) and reject invalid combinations
- [ ] Optional `host_allow` binding:
  - accept canonical hostnames only
  - reject IP literals when policy requires
  - enforce canonicalization rules consistent with NetworkConstraints

### 2) Capability gating (unit)
- [ ] Allowed: invoking capability includes `CredentialId` in `credential_allow`
- [ ] Denied: missing `credential_allow` entry -> DecisionReceipt with reason_code + evidence
- [ ] Denied: credential present but destination host not allowed by CredentialObject binding

### 3) Egress proxy injection (integration, deterministic)
- [ ] HTTP header injection:
  - proxy injects `Authorization` (or configured header) only when allowed
  - connector never sees secret bytes
- [ ] Host mismatch deny:
  - correct denial at the proxy boundary
  - structured logs include correlation_id + reason_code
- [ ] Replay/nonce safety when applicable (e.g., timestamped signatures)

### 4) SecretObject access + zeroize discipline (adversarial)
- [ ] Secret access requires a valid `SecretAccessToken`
- [ ] Every access emits `AuditEvent` `event_type="secret.access"` with redacted metadata
- [ ] Verify zeroization discipline:
  - no secret bytes in logs
  - no secret bytes in error strings
  - panic-safe zeroization paths (Drop-based)

## Golden Vectors
Location: `tests/vectors/credentials/`
- `credential_object_valid.cbor`
- `credential_object_invalid.cbor`
- `credential_allow_allow.cbor`
- `credential_allow_deny.cbor`

## Logging Requirements
- MUST use structured JSON logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.
- MUST NOT log secret bytes; log only stable IDs (`SecretId`, `CredentialId`) and redacted metadata.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Test suite is deterministic and runs in CI.
- Denials produce DecisionReceipts with correct reason codes and evidence.
- Secret bytes never appear in logs, errors, or artifacts.

","notes":"## Session 2026-01-17: Test Implementation Progress

### Implemented
1. **CapabilityConstraints credential_allow enforcement** (capability.rs)
   - Added `is_credential_allowed()` method
   - Added `validate_credential()` method returning CredentialValidationError
   - 7 unit tests covering allow/deny scenarios

2. **IP literal detection and host policy validation** (credential.rs)
   - Added `is_ip_literal()` static method (handles IPv4, IPv6, ports)
   - Added `has_ip_literal_in_host_allow()` method
   - Added `validate_host_policy(reject_ip_literals)` method
   - 12 unit tests covering IPv4/IPv6 detection and policy enforcement

3. **CredentialId canonicity tests** (credential.rs)
   - Format validation (36 chars, 4 hyphens, lowercase)
   - Stable display verification
   - Case-insensitive parsing tests

4. **Golden vectors** (tests/credential_golden_vectors.rs)
   - credential_object_valid.cbor
   - credential_object_host_bound.cbor
   - credential_allow_constraints.cbor
   - 18 tests covering validation, capability gating, host binding, zeroize discipline

### Blocked
- **Egress proxy injection tests**: Blocked by kt9r (egress proxy implementation)
- **AuditEvent emission tests**: Need audit infrastructure from kt9r

### Test Results
- All 41 credential-related unit tests pass
- All 18 golden vector tests pass
- Full fcp-core test suite passes","status":"in_progress","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:25:09.606885236-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T00:44:13.931651688-05:00","labels":["fcp2","mvp","secrets","security","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nnja","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-kt9r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:25:16.540583365-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-nnja","depends_on_id":"flywheel_connectors-1n78.35","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-15T03:25:16.632327144-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nuc","title":"fcp.twilio: Verify 2FA authentication service","description":"# fcp.twilio: Verify 2FA authentication service (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Twilio Verify to support 2FA/verification workflows:
- send verification codes
- check verification codes

This surface is security-sensitive and must be tightly capability scoped.

## Scope
- Create a verification (send code via SMS/call/etc).
- Check a verification (validate code).
- Optional: cancel verification.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `twilio.verify.send` (dangerous-ish: sends messages)
- `twilio.verify.check` (auth-sensitive)
- Optional: `twilio.verify.cancel`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- PII hygiene:
  - phone numbers are sensitive (never log)
  - verification codes are secrets (never log)
- For sends:
  - receipts/audit events include verification SID and destination hash only

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `twilio.verify.send`
  - `twilio.verify.check`
  - Optional: `twilio.verify.cancel`
- Strict validation:
  - code length/format
  - channel allowlist

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - send/check flows
  - redaction (no codes/numbers in logs)
  - error mapping (invalid code, expired, rate limited)

## Acceptance criteria
- Verify operations are mechanically authorized, safe, and do not leak secrets.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-nvt","title":"fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management","description":"# fcp.sentry: Issue assignment and status management (FCP2)

## Goal
Allow safe, auditable manipulation of Sentry issue workflow state:
- assign/unassign
- resolve/unresolve
- ignore/unignore
- set priority (if supported)

These are write operations that can affect on-call workflows.

## Scope
### Write operations
- `sentry.issue.assign`
- `sentry.issue.resolve`
- `sentry.issue.ignore`
- `sentry.issue.update_priority`

### Read support
- fetch minimal issue metadata required for optimistic concurrency (if available).

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `sentry.issues.write`

Policy guidance:
- Some orgs may require approvals for resolving/ignoring issues.

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- For every write:
  - emit OperationReceipt + AuditEvent
  - include issue id + action + correlation id
  - never include raw issue payloads in logs

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `sentry.issue.assign`
  - `sentry.issue.set_status` (resolve/ignore/unresolve)
  - `sentry.issue.set_priority`
- Denial reason codes for policy engine.

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - capability gating
  - approval gating (when policy requires)
  - error mapping (permission denied, conflict)
  - audit/receipt emission

## Acceptance criteria
- Issue workflow actions are safe, explainable, and auditable.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ofw","title":"fcp.gmail: Google Gmail API Connector","description":"# fcp.gmail - Gmail API Connector (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide an FCP2-compliant Gmail connector for email automation with strict zone isolation, sandboxing, and mechanical authorization.

## Manifest archetypes (closed set)
- `operational`
- `streaming` (optional: watch/push or polling-driven event stream)

## Zone model

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The *"zones"* described in this section are a **recommended deployment pattern** (run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies) and/or conceptual risk tiers.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

- Single-zone bound: one connector instance ↔ one `ZoneId`.
- Recommended: `z:private:gmail:<account>` (policy-owned naming).

## External surface
### NetworkConstraints
Default-deny; allow only the Gmail/Google API endpoints required by:
- Gmail API
- OAuth token refresh (if used by the OAuth library)

### Credential injection
- OAuth2 refresh/access tokens via `CredentialId`.
- Never persist tokens to disk.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `gmail.messages.read`
- `gmail.messages.send`
- `gmail.messages.modify` (labels/trash)
- `gmail.threads.read` / `gmail.threads.modify`
- `gmail.labels.manage`
- `gmail.settings.manage` (Dangerous)

## Operations (representative)
- messages: get/list/send/reply/forward/modify/trash/delete
- threads: get/list/modify/trash
- labels: list/create/update/delete
- drafts: create/get/list/update/send/delete

## Eventing (optional)
If we support push/watch:
- connector must not expose inbound ports directly
- integrate through `fcp-host` controlled ingress (or deterministic polling)

## State model
- Stateless for simple reads.
- For incremental sync (historyId), persist cursor in connector state and advance under a singleton-writer lease.

## Tests
- Mock-only unit/integration tests with wiremock.
- Deterministic historyId/poll simulation.
- E2E compliance run validates default deny + NetworkConstraints + state/cursor behavior.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Passes connector compliance runner.
- Deterministic tests; no live Gmail calls in unit tests.
